title: AWS Pricing Calculator Service Catalog Entry

serviceCode.description: Canonical service code from manifest/en_US.json (e.g. aWSLambda, ec2Enhancement).

templateId.description: The estimateFor value the calculator expects. Optional for parent envelopes whose children carry their own template ids.

status.description: verified = round-trip test passes; partial = saves+renders but with Required-input; unverified = not yet probed; broken = known to fail.

lastVerifiedAt.description: ISO date of the most recent successful round-trip. Required when status is 'verified' or 'partial'.

verifiedEstimateId.description: SHA1 estimate id from the most recent successful round-trip. Required when status is 'verified' or 'partial'.

traps.description: Human-readable gotchas an agent or author should know. Free-form prose.

subServices.description: For parent envelopes only — describes each child's serviceCode, estimateFor, and required fields.

minimalConfig.description: The smallest config that produces an editable estimate. For parent envelopes, keyed by sub-service code; for plain services, the config object itself.

defaultFields.description: Per-field literal saved-blob shapes injected at add_service time when the agent omits the key. Use for fields the calculator silently demands but the manifest does NOT mark validations.required (the case where get_service_fields doesn't expose them and the lint can't refuse without them). Authors declare the canonical envelope; lib/handler-helpers.js#applyDefaultFields merges any missing keys into the user's config before estimate.addService.

productCodes.description: Manifest service codes (subType:'subService') that route under this provider but are not in the parent's templates[]. Lets the lint hint redirect agents from a product code (e.g. titanTextEmbeddingsV2) to the provider code (e.g. amazon) that the calculator's parent envelope actually claims.
